title: Epoxy Resin Degree-of-Cure Monitor and Controller
description: Closed-loop cure monitor that heats an epoxy laminate sample on a Peltier stage, tracks degree-of-cure α in real time via dielectric ion viscosity measurement, holds cure temperature via PID, and triggers cool-down at α ≥ 0.95 while logging to serial for host-side analysis.
objective: Achieve full cure (α ≥ 0.95) with peak temperature ≤ 80 °C; detect gelation/vitrification transitions; log temperature, α, and actuator state every 2 s; fault-cut on overtemperature.
constraints: Arduino Nano; Peltier TEC; interdigitated DEA sensor on FR4; BOM < $35; serial logging only.
supporting_fact_titles: Dielectric Cure Monitoring Ion Viscosity and Degree of Cure
element_usage_notes: Measures ion viscosity change during cure; ADC reading log-scaled to IV
Bidirectional temperature control for precise isothermal cure profile
Reads cure temperature as PID process variable; Steinhart-Hart in firmware
Streams CSV to host PC at 115200 baud for off-line analysis

element_interactions.description: PID uses thermistor reading as process variable to modulate Peltier duty cycle
Computed α value streamed to host for real-time cure curve plotting
Temperature co-logged with α for correlation analysis
Actuator state logged for energy audit
